methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Built Robotics, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $137M to date, operates within a competitive frontier-tech sector anchored by nine same-stage and adjacent-stage private peers, alongside one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ). The peer set ranges from early-stage players valued at $300M to mature positions at $10.0B, with named comparables including 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C), and Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B).

Two valuation methods are applicable. The stage-progression approach, using total raised as a proxy base ($4M), applies a 1.7x Series C forward multiplier, yielding $7M. The peer-median method across nine robotics comparables yields a $1.6B reference point, with the peer cohort distributed across a $300M–$10.0B range. These methods triangulate to a low of $6M, a median of $803M, and a high of $1.8B.

The wide dispersion between methods reflects genuine uncertainty in frontier-tech valuation. The stage-progression floor ($7M) appears disconnected from peer-set norms and likely underweights Built Robotics' capital deployment relative to sector standards. A reasonable working range is $800M–$1.8B, centered near the peer median, with the key uncertainty being revenue traction and path to profitability relative to the named peer set.
